name: Apache Airflow
description: Airflow plugin allows you to seamlessly run Airflow tasks in the Flyte workflow without changing any code.

name: Async FSSpec
description: The Flyte async fsspec plugin is a powerful addition to the Flyte ecosystem designed to optimize the performance of object transmission. This plugin focuses on overriding key methods of the file systems in fsspec to introduce efficiency improvements, resulting in accelerated data transfers between Flyte workflows and object storage.

name: AWS Athena
description: Flyte backend can be connected with Athena. Once enabled, it allows you to query AWS Athena service (Presto + ANSI SQL Support) and retrieve typed schema (optionally).

name: AWS Batch
description: Flyte backend can be connected with AWS batch. Once enabled, it allows you to run flyte task on AWS batch service

name: AWS SageMaker
description: The plugin currently features a SageMaker deployment connector.

name: Bigquery
description: BigQuery enables us to build data-intensive applications without operational burden. Flyte backend can be connected with the BigQuery service. Once enabled, it can allow you to query a BigQuery table.

name: BigQuery
description: This plugin provides BigQuery integration for Flyte, enabling you to run BigQuery queries as Flyte tasks.

name: Comet ML
description: Comet’s machine learning platform integrates with your existing infrastructure and tools so you can manage, visualize, and optimize models—from training runs to production monitoring. This plugin integrates Flyte with Comet.ml by configuring links between the two platforms.

name: Dask
description: Flyte can execute dask jobs natively on a Kubernetes Cluster, which manages the virtual dask cluster's lifecycle

name: Dask
description: Flyte can execute dask jobs natively on a Kubernetes Cluster, which manages the virtual dask cluster's lifecycle

name: Databricks
description: This plugin provides Databricks integration for Flyte, enabling you to run Spark jobs on Databricks as Flyte tasks.

name: DBT
description: Flytekit plugin for performing DBT tasks. Currently it supports  dbt run , dbt test, dbt source freshness tasks.

name: Deck
description: This plugin provides additional renderers to improve task visibility within Flytekit.

name: Dgxc-lepton
description: A professional Flytekit plugin that enables seamless deployment and management of AI inference endpoints using Lepton AI infrastructure within Flyte workflows.

name: Dolt
description: The DoltTable plugin is a wrapper that uses Dolt to move data between pandas.DataFrame’s at execution time and database tables at rest.

name: DuckDB
description: Run analytical workloads with ease using DuckDB.

name: Envd
description: envd is a command-line tool that helps you create the container-based development environment for AI/ML.

name: Flyte Interactive
description: > FlyteInteractive = Develop Flyte with the speed of flying

name: FSSpec
description: This plugin provides an implementation of the data persistence layer in Flytekit that uses fsspec. Once this plugin

name: Geopandas
description: GeoPandas GeoPandas is an open source project to make working with geospatial data in python easier.

name: Google IAP
description: GCP Identity Aware Proxy (IAP) is a managed Google Cloud Platform (GCP) service that makes it easy to protect applications deployed on GCP by verifying user identity and using context to determine whether a user should be granted access. Because requests to applications protected with IAP first have to pass IAP before they can reach the protected backends, IAP provides a convenient way to implement a zero-trust access model.

name: Great Expectations
description: Great Expectations helps enforce data quality. The plugin supports the usage of Great Expectations as task and type.

name: Hive
description: Flyte backend can be connected with various Hive services. Once enabled, it allows you to query a Hive service (e.g., Qubole) and retrieve typed schema (optionally).

name: Hugging Face
description: Hugging Face is a community and data science platform that provides: Tools that enable users to build, train and deploy ML models based on open source (OS) code and technologies

name: Inference
description: Serve models natively in Flyte tasks using inference providers like NIM, Ollama, and others.

name: Kubeflow MPI
description: This plugin uses the Kubeflow MPI Operator and provides an extremely simplified interface for executing distributed training.

name: Kubeflow PyTorch
description: This plugin uses the Kubeflow PyTorch Operator and provides an extremely simplified interface for executing distributed training using various PyTorch backends.

name: Kubeflow TensorFlow
description: This plugin uses the Kubeflow TensorFlow Operator and provides an extremely simplified interface for executing distributed training using various TensorFlow backends.

name: Kubernetes Pod
description: > [!IMPORTANT]

name: Kubernetes Stateful
description: This plugin provides support for Kubernetes StatefulSet and Service integration, enabling seamless provisioning and coordination with any Kubernetes services or Flyte tasks. It is especially suited for deep learning use cases at scale, where distributed and parallelized data loading and caching across nodes are required.

name: Memory Machine Cloud
description: Flyte Connector plugin to allow executing Flyte tasks using MemVerge Memory Machine Cloud.

name: Memray Profiling
description: Memray tracks and reports memory allocations, both in python code and in compiled extension modules.

name: MLflow
description: MLflow enables us to log parameters, code, and results in machine learning experiments and compare them using an interactive UI.

name: Modin
description: Modin is a pandas-accelerator that helps handle large datasets. It is a light-weight extension that is similar to the pandas API. It uses the concept of parallelism to reduce overhead, and improve the performance of pandas operations by leveraging the compute resources available.

name: Neptune
description: Neptune is the MLOps stack component for experiment tracking. It offers a single place to log, compare, store, and collaborate on experiments and models. This plugin integrates Flyte with Neptune by configuring links between the two platforms.

name: OmegaConf
description: Flytekit python natively supports serialization of many data types for exchanging information between tasks.

name: ONNX PyTorch
description: This plugin allows you to generate ONNX models from your PyTorch models.

name: ONNX ScikitLearn
description: This plugin allows you to generate ONNX models from your ScikitLearn models.

name: ONNX TensorFlow
description: This plugin allows you to generate ONNX models from your TensorFlow Keras models.

name: OpenAI
description: The plugin currently features ChatGPT and Batch API connectors.

name: OpenAI
description: This plugin provides a drop-in replacement for OpenAI packages. It provides

name: Optuna (wrapper)
description: This documentation provides a guide to a fully parallelized Flyte plugin for Optuna. This wrapper leverages Flyte's scalable and distributed workflow orchestration capabilities to parallelize Optuna's hyperparameter optimization across multiple trials efficiently.

name: Pandera
description: Flytekit python natively supports many data types, including a FlyteSchema type for type-annotating pandas DataFrames. The Flytekit Pandera plugin provides an alternative for defining DataFrame schemas by integrating with Pandera, a runtime data validation tool for pandas DataFrames.

name: Papermill
description: It is possible to run a Jupyter notebook as a Flyte task using Papermill. Papermill executes the notebook as a whole, so before using this plugin, it is essential to construct your notebook as recommended by Papermill.

name: Perian Job Platform
description: Flyte Connector plugin for executing Flyte tasks on Perian Job Platform (perian.io).

modules.name: PerianConfig
modules.description: PerianConfig from Perian Job Platform plugin

modules.name: PerianContainerTask
modules.description: PerianContainerTask from Perian Job Platform plugin

modules.name: PerianTask
modules.description: PerianTask from Perian Job Platform plugin

name: polars
description: This plugin provides native support for **Polars DataFrames and LazyFrames** in Flyte, enabling efficient data processing with Polars' high-performance DataFrame library.

name: Polars
description: Polars is a blazingly fast DataFrames library implemented in Rust using Apache Arrow Columnar Format as memory model.

name: pytorch
description: Union can execute **PyTorch distributed training jobs** natively on a Kubernetes Cluster, which manages the lifecycle of worker pods, rendezvous coordination, spin-up, and tear down. It leverages the open-sourced **TorchElastic (torch.distributed.elastic)** launcher and the **Kubeflow PyTorch Operator**, enabling fault-tolerant and elastic training across multiple nodes.

name: Ray
description: Flyte backend can be connected with Ray. Once enabled, it allows you to run flyte task on Ray cluster

name: Ray
description: Union can execute Ray jobs natively on a Kubernetes Cluster,

name: SGLang
description: Serve large language models using SGLang with Flyte Apps.

name: Slurm
description: The Slurm connector is designed to integrate Flyte workflows with Slurm-managed high-performance computing (HPC) clusters, enabling users to leverage Slurm's capability of compute resource allocation, scheduling, and monitoring.

name: Snowflake
description: Snowflake enables us to build data-intensive applications without operational burden. Flyte backend can be connected with the Snowflake service. Once enabled, it can allow you to query a Snowflake service.

name: Snowflake
description: This plugin provides Snowflake integration for Flyte, enabling you to run Snowflake queries as Flyte tasks.

name: Spark
description: Flyte can execute Spark jobs natively on a Kubernetes Cluster, which manages a virtual cluster’s lifecycle, spin-up, and tear down. It leverages the open-sourced Spark On K8s Operator and can be enabled without signing up for any service. This is like running a transient spark cluster — a type of cluster spun up for a specific Spark job and torn down after completion.

name: Spark
description: Union can execute Spark jobs natively on a Kubernetes Cluster, which manages a virtual cluster’s lifecycle, spin-up, and tear down. It leverages the open-sourced Spark On K8s Operator and can be enabled without signing up for any service. This is like running a transient spark cluster — a type of cluster spun up for a specific Spark job and torn down after completion.

name: SQLAlchemy
description: SQLAlchemy is the Python SQL toolkit and Object Relational Mapper that gives application developers the full power and flexibility of SQL. Flyte provides an easy-to-use interface to utilize SQLAlchemy to connect to various SQL Databases.

name: Vaex
description: Vaex is a high-performance Python library for lazy out-of-core DataFrames

name: vLLM
description: Serve large language models using vLLM with Flyte Apps.

name: Weights & Biases
description: The Weights and Biases MLOps platform helps AI developers streamline their ML workflow from end-to-end. This plugin

name: Weights & Biases
description: This plugin provides integration between Flyte and Weights & Biases (W&B) for experiment tracking, including support for distributed training with PyTorch Elastic.

name: whylogs
description: whylogs is an open source library for logging any kind of data. With whylogs,
